title: Anguina tricuspidata
taxonomy: Anguina tricuspidata is a type of nematode, specifically a species of Anguina within the family Anguinidae. Anguina tricuspidata is classified as a parasitic plant nematode, which is a type of microscopic worm that feeds on plants. It belongs to the kingdom Animalia, phylum Nematoda, class Secernentea, and order Tylenchida. Anguina tricuspidata is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species. This nematode is known to be a parasite of certain grasses and sedges.
